news.headline: US asset manager CrossingBridge Advisors, LLC partners with Nordic debt manager NCI Advisory A/S; deal announced June 28, 2024.
news.summary: On June 28, 2024, Pleasantville, NY-based CrossingBridge Advisors, LLC revealed a significant collaboration with Denmark's top debt asset management company, NCI Advisory A/S. This partnership marks a pivotal move for CrossingBridge, signaling an alliance with a high-performing firm. David Sherman, CEO of CrossingBridge, expressed excitement regarding the newly formed strategic alliance with NCI Advisory.

news.headline: Lindsay Corp beats earning estimate with $1.41 EPS, Q3 revenue at $139M, down 15.4%Y/Y; backlog at $246.9M.
news.summary: Lindsay Corporation (NYSE: LNN) reported adjusted earnings per share of $1.41 for the third quarter of fiscal 2024, exceeding the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.25. However, this was an 8% decline from the previous year. With an income tax credit of 44 cents per share included, earnings per share for the quarter were $1.85. Revenues were $139 million, down 15.4% from the year-ago quarter and missing the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $152 million. The backlog as of May 31, 2024, stood at $246.9 million, a significant increase from $94.5 million in the previous year.Cost of operating revenues dropped by 16.7% to $93 million, while gross profit decreased by 12.8% to $46.5 million. The gross margin was 33.4%. Operating expenses were up 1.1% year over year to $26.6 million, resulting in an operating income of $20 million, down from $27 million in the prior year.In the Irrigation segment, revenues fell by 19% to approximately $115 million, with North America irrigation revenues decreasing by 9% to $68 million.

news.headline: Exro Technologies reported 2024 Annual General Meeting voting results in Calgary, AB. Stock symbols: TSX: EXRO, OTCQB: EXROF.
news.summary: In Calgary, Alberta on June 28, 2024, Exro Technologies Inc. (TSX: EXRO) (OTCQB: EXROF) announced its position as a prominent clean-tech firm offering exclusive propulsion system technology for e-mobility and specialized battery control technology for stationary energy purposes.

news.headline: Apogee Enterprises, Inc. beat Q1 fiscal 2025 EPS estimates with $1.44 and revenue of $331.5 million, down 8.3% YoY.
news.summary: Apogee Enterprises, Inc. (NASDAQ: APOG) reported earnings per share of $1.44 in first-quarter fiscal 2025, exceeding the Zacks Consensus Estimate of $1.16. This marked a 37.1% increase from the previous year. However, the company's revenues for the quarter dropped 8.3% year over year to $331.5 million due to lower volume. Cost of sales decreased by 13.4% to $233 million, while gross profit rose by 6.3% to $99 million with an expanded gross margin of 29.8%. Operating income increased by 22.5% to $41 million, with selling, general, and administrative expenses decreasing by 2.9% to $57 million. In the Architectural Framing Systems segment, revenues declined by 18.9% to $133 million, with operating profit at $18.4 million compared to $19.9 million in the previous year. The lower volumes were partly offset by favorable material costs, mix, and productivity gains.

news.headline: Kellanova (NYSE: K) upgraded to Zacks Rank #2 with positive earnings outlook, impacting stock prices significantly.
news.summary: Kellanova (NYSE: K) has recently been upgraded to a Zacks Rank #2 (Buy) due to an upward trend in earnings estimates. The Zacks rating system relies on EPS estimates from sell-side analysts, indicating a positive earnings outlook for Kellanova. This rating upgrade suggests potential buying pressure and a rise in stock price. The Zacks rating system emphasizes the impact of changing earnings pictures on stock prices, making it a valuable tool for individual investors compared to subjective analyst upgrades. The full story is available on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Sharpen launches Usable AI™ platform revolutionizing customer service in contact centers, JUNE 28, 2024.
news.summary: Indianapolis, the 28th of June in 2024 - Sharpen, a prominent provider of cloud contact center software, unveiled a groundbreaking development in the customer service sector by introducing their Usable AI™ platform. This innovative AI-based platform is poised to revolutionize contact centers by offering cutting-edge solutions to enhance customer interactions.

news.headline: Amazon Labor Union and Teamsters, with 1.3 million members, join forces to challenge Amazon, signaling escalation in battle.
news.summary: The partnership between the Amazon Labor Union and the Teamsters, a union with 1.3 million members, represents a significant development in their efforts to confront the online retail giant.

news.headline: Abacus Life plans public offering, 10,000,000 shares of common stock, 30-day option for additional 1,500,000 shares.
news.summary: Orlando, Florida - June 13, 2024 - Abacus Life, Inc. (NASDAQ: ABL), a leader in alternative asset management focusing on longevity and actuarial technology, has revealed the launch of an underwritten public offering. The offering includes 10,000,000 shares of its common stock, with an additional option for the underwriters to purchase up to 1,500,000 more shares within 30 days at the public offering price. Abacus is the seller of all shares in the public offering.

news.headline: Arm Holdings plc will join the Nasdaq-100 Index on June 24, 2024, replacing Sirius XM Holdings Inc. in various indices.
news.summary: On June 13, 2024, Nasdaq announced that Arm Holdings plc (Nasdaq: ARM) will be included in multiple Nasdaq indices, including the Nasdaq-100 Index® (Nasdaq: NDX®), the Nasdaq-100 Equal Weighted™ Index (Nasdaq: NDXE™), the Nasdaq-100 Tech Sector™ Index (Nasdaq: NDXT™), the Nasdaq-100 Technology Sector Market-Cap Weighted™ Index (Nasdaq: NDXTMC™), and the Nasdaq-100 Technology Sector Adjusted Market-Cap Weighted™ Index (Nasdaq: NDXT10™). This change is set to take effect before the market opens on June 24, 2024. Arm Holdings plc will replace Sirius XM Holdings Inc. (Nasdaq: SIRI) in the Nasdaq-100 Index® and the Nasdaq-100 Equal Weighted™ Index. Additionally, Sirius XM will be removed from the Nasdaq-100 Ex-Tech Sector™ Index (Nasdaq: NDXX™), the Nasdaq-100 ESG Index (Nasdaq: NDXESG™), the Nasdaq-100 Sustainable ESG Select Index (Nasdaq: NDXSES™), and the Nasdaq-100 Target 25 Index® (Nasdaq: NDXT25™) on the same date.

news.headline: Namib Minerals to go public by merging with Hennessy Capital Investment Corp. VI, expect to close deal in Q4 of 2024.
news.summary: On June 18, 2024, Namib Minerals, a prominent gold mining company in sub-Saharan Africa, announced a definitive agreement with Hennessy Capital Investment Corp. VI (Nasdaq: HCVI), a special purpose acquisition company listed on Nasdaq, for a business combination. The agreement, expected to be finalized in the fourth quarter of 2024, is contingent upon customary closing conditions and approvals. The merged entity, to be known as Namib Minerals once the transaction is completed, plans to list its common stock and warrants on Nasdaq under the ticker symbols "NAMM" and "NAMMW," pending listing application approval.

news.headline: April 2024 saw a 5.7% increase in monthly mortgage payments and a 0.1% rise in single-family home prices, impacting affordability.
news.summary: The affordability of housing in April 2024 was challenged due to a rise in mortgage rates and higher home prices. In comparison to March 2024, the average monthly mortgage payment surged by 5.7%. Additionally, the median price for single-family homes saw a slight uptick of 0.1%. These changes indicate the increasing financial burden on potential homebuyers looking to enter the market.

news.headline: Carnival Soars as Analysts Raise Price Targets after Q2 Earnings Beat. Stock up 2% to $18.18. Goldman Sachs hikes target to $23 for 29% rise.思Analyzed strong booking trends and pricing momentum. Rise in new-to-cruise passengers by 10% year-over-year in 2024. Booking demand not driven by pent-up demand but brand appeal and better yield management.
news.summary: Investment bank analysts raised Carnival Corp.'s year-ahead price targets following a strong earnings report and optimistic guidance. Shares rose 2% to $18.18, aiming for the seventh consecutive session of gains, the longest streak since December 2023. Goldman Sachs upgraded the price target from $22 to $23, suggesting a 29% upside. They highlighted a 10% year-over-year increase in new-to-cruise passengers in 2024. The company's improved yield management and brand consideration led to higher ticket prices, not just pent-up demand. Analysts believe consumer concerns after a slight Q1 decline in onboard spending should now ease.

news.headline: Cano Health successfully emerges from Chapter 11 with $290 million in annual cost reductions by 2024.
news.summary: Cano Health, Inc. has successfully completed a court-supervised restructuring process, resulting in a reduced debt burden and securing new financing for ongoing operations. The company aims to achieve annual cost reductions of $290 million by the end of 2024. The announcement was made on June 28, 2024 in Miami.

news.headline: States are introducing bills to regulate credit card swipe fees, posing challenges for banks and credit card companies.
news.summary: Financial institutions and credit card companies are facing challenges from emerging state legislation targeting swipe fees in various states across the country. This poses a significant concern for banks as they navigate the potential impact on their revenue streams from these new regulations. The industry is closely monitoring the situation as lawmakers debate the issue, with the outcome likely to have widespread ramifications for businesses and consumers alike.

news.headline: Red Cat (Nasdaq: RCAT) to join Russell Microcap® Index post-June 28, 2024, per addition list on May 24, 2024.
news.summary: In San Juan, Puerto Rico on June 24, 2024, Red Cat Holdings, Inc. (Nasdaq: RCAT), a drone technology company specializing in robotic hardware and software for military, government, and commercial sectors, revealed its inclusion in the Russell Microcap® Index. This will occur after the 2024 Russell US Indexes annual reconstitution, becoming effective after the closure of US equity markets on Friday, June 28, 2024. The decision was based on a preliminary list of additions released on Friday, May 24, 2024.

news.headline: Metro areas are building more houses than before due to factors like economic growth and population increase.
news.summary: Metropolitan areas are seeing a rise in housing production beyond their usual levels. The surge in construction is due to factors like economic expansion, population growth, and initiatives for urban development. This trend indicates a significant increase in housing projects within these regions, signaling potential opportunities for residents and investors.

news.headline: Michael Jackson passed away with a staggering $500 million debt, including $40 million owed to A.E.G. in 2009, now fully settled.
news.summary: In 2009, Jackson reportedly owed approximately $40 million to tour promoter A.E.G., as stated by his estate’s executors in a court document. However, these executors have confirmed that all the debts have now been cleared.

news.headline: Biden rejects calls to withdraw from 2024 race at Raleigh rally.
Earth Science Tech, Inc. reported annual revenue of over $11.9M for the year ended March 31, 2024. #ETST #businessnews
news.summary: In Miami, FL on June 28, 2024, Earth Science Tech, Inc. (OTC: ETST) disclosed its financial outcomes and key achievements for the year closing on March 31, 2024. ETST, a holding company concentrating on health and wellness, provided insights into its performance during this period.

news.headline: Cryptocurrency prices remain stagnant as U.S. inflation eases. Analysts puzzled over why Bitcoin and altcoins have not rallied despite this.
news.summary: Analysts and traders have long anticipated that a decrease in inflation would boost the crypto market's performance. Despite this expectation, cryptocurrency prices continue to remain low. Cointelegraph provides insights into the reasons behind this phenomenon.

news.headline: Hong Kong plans to boost DeFi presence, as DAO allocates $37M for Julian Assange's cause in Finance Redefined.
news.summary: The Hong Kong government is looking to strengthen its position in the financial technology (fintech) sector by focusing on decentralized finance (DeFi) and the metaverse. Despite facing obstacles in the current environment, the government is determined to achieve this goal. Hong Kong plans to invest significant resources and expertise into these areas to establish itself as a leading force in DeFi and the metaverse. This strategic move is part of the government's broader plan to maintain its dominance in the fintech industry.

news.headline: CFTC reported positions for the week ending June 25th.
Biden faces challenges in debate with Trump he requested.
Le Pen's group gains momentum in French poll of polls, leading the way. #Bloomberg #LePen
Nokia Corp bought back stocks on 06/28/2024.
Goldman Sachs predicts a recovery for commercial real estate market with challenges ahead, after 8 quarters of declining transactions.
news.summary: A recent report from Goldman Sachs, shared by analyst Caitlin Burrows, suggests that the worst may be over for commercial real estate (CRE) assets but a full recovery is still distant. While the annual decline in CRE transaction volumes seems to have bottomed out, challenges remain for a widespread rebound. The comparison to the Global Financial Crisis shows that after eight quarters of decline, a positive shift occurred in the ninth quarter. Currently, CRE has experienced eight quarters of declines starting from Q3 2022. Some cities show exceptions to the overall trend. Leading indicators hint at a bottoming out of the CRE market. One positive sign is the OECD's U.S. Composite Business Confidence Index. Despite these positive indications, a full recovery for CRE assets is still a ways off.

news.headline: Basic Fun, Inc. in Boca Raton, FL, strengthens financial structure for future success in toy market.

news.headline: SLB wins contract for all-electric subsea project in Norway for 12-well system in Fram Sor field; focusing on electrification for production efficiency.
news.summary: SLB has secured a contract from Equinor ASA to complete the front-end engineering design ("FEED") for a groundbreaking all-electric subsea project in Norway. This initiative demonstrates a significant progression in subsea technology by emphasizing electrification for improved efficiency and production capabilities. The project will be managed by SLB OneSubsea, a collaborative effort between SLB, Aker Solutions, and Subsea7, headquartered in Oslo and Houston. Under the agreement, SLB will lead the FEED for a 12-well, all-electric subsea production systems endeavor situated in the Fram Sor field off the coast of Norway. Following the final investment decision, SLB OneSubsea will be directly appointed for the subsequent engineering, procurement, and construction phases. The Fram Sor project will employ SLB OneSubsea's standard subsea tree design, enhanced with a fully electrified power, control, and actuation system. By replacing high-pressure hydraulic systems with electric alternatives, operators will have the capability to expand exploration opportunities.

news.headline: Homewatch CareGivers in Colo. wins 3 Innovation Awards from Franchise Update Media for Total Care Solutions™️.
news.summary: Homewatch CareGivers, based in Greenwood Village, Colorado, has recently been recognized with three esteemed awards at Franchise Update Media's sixth annual Franchise Innovation Award contest. The awards highlight the company's innovative approach with Homewatch CareGivers Total Care Solutions™️. This announcement was made on June 28, 2024.

news.headline: Monday FX Option Expiries

Cryptocurrency prices dipped, Bitcoin at $60,719.49 (-1.7%), Ethereum at $3,378.62 (-2%), Dogecoin at $0.1237 (-0.8%). Positive year-end run expected by trader. Bolivia legalized Bitcoin after lifting decade-long ban. Bitcoin miners' selling pressure easing.
news.summary: Cryptocurrency markets have taken a downturn despite U.S. inflation remaining steady in May. Prices for major cryptocurrencies are as follows: Bitcoin (BTC) is at $60,719.49, down 1.7%; Ethereum (ETH) at $3,378.62, down 2%; Solana (SOL) at $141.47, down 4.8%; Dogecoin (DOGE) at $0.1237, down 0.8%; and Shiba Inu (SHIB) at $0.00001704, down 2.8%.Noteworthy statistics include a 74% increase in large transaction volume and a 13.8% rise in daily active addresses, with a significant 704.6% spike in exchanges netflows. Additionally, 35,808 traders were liquidated for $112 million in the past 24 hours, and Bitcoin withdrawals from miner-affiliated wallets dropped by 85% post-April halving. Bolivia legalized Bitcoin after a decade-long ban, allowing financial institutions to use cryptocurrency payments officially.In notable developments, a Bitcoin-powered core blockchain has launched an exchange-traded product in Sweden. For more details, visit Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Income investors focus on dividends for consistent cash flow. Alexandria Real Estate Equities is a top dividend stock.
news.summary: Income investors prioritize consistent cash flow from their liquid investments, with dividends playing a critical role as a reliable source of income. Dividends are distributions of a company's earnings paid to shareholders, measured by dividend yield as a percentage of the current stock price. Research indicates dividends often contribute over one-third of total returns in the long run. Alexandria Real Estate Equities, headquartered in Pasadena, is spotlighted for its dividend performance. Investors seek high returns from various securities like stocks, bonds, and ETFs to maximize portfolio gains.

news.headline: Motorola launches VESTA NXT to enhance emergency response with AI, facilitating quick verification and analysis of 9-1-1 calls.
news.summary: Motorola Solutions, Inc. has launched VESTA NXT, an AI-enabled 9-1-1 software to help improve the efficiency of call handlers and dispatchers. The software addresses challenges faced by telecommunicators dealing with large amounts of information from varied sources that need quick analysis. It also tackles the issue of non-emergency calls diverting attention from critical emergencies. VESTA NXT offers features like call transcription, translation, and a virtual assistant for non-emergency calls. Available on cloud and on-premise, it provides a consistent user experience regardless of deployment. This software aims to enhance response times and streamline emergency services for better outcomes.

news.headline: Eni sells Nikaitchuq and Oooguruk assets to Hilcorp in Alaska. Enhanced production by 7,000 Bo/d. Strategic move for portfolio balance.
news.summary: Eni SpA has signed a binding agreement to sell its Nikaitchuq and Oooguruk upstream offshore assets in Alaska to Hilcorp. Eni held a 30% interest in Oooguruk field but later acquired the remaining stake. Eni's production capabilities in Alaska increased by 7,000 Bo/d due to this acquisition. Eni was already the operator with a 100% working interest in Nikaitchuq field. Their broader strategy includes generating a net portfolio inflow of €8 billion over 2024-2027 through divesting non-strategic assets.

news.headline: Market Street Memory Care Residence Palm Coast celebrates 6th year as a Great Place to Work® under Watercrest Senior Living.
news.summary: In Vero Beach, Florida, as of June 28, 2024, Market Street Memory Care Residence Palm Coast has been recognized as a Great Place to Work® for the sixth consecutive year. This certification comes under the operational management of Watercrest Senior Living. This prestigious designation highlights the commitment to excellence in providing quality care and services to residents.

news.headline: Garmin launches Edge 1050 smart cycling computer with improved battery life, navigation, and safety features. Aimed at enhancing cyclists' riding experience, it includes features like LiveTrack and Varia compatibility. Garmin aims to provide an advanced riding experience by offering road hazard alerts, in-ride messaging, and leaderboards during group rides. This launch expands Garmin's offerings for cyclists.
news.summary: Garmin has introduced the Edge 1050 smart cycling computer to enhance its fitness offerings. The device offers improved battery life, navigation, performance tracking, cycling awareness, and connectivity. It includes safety features like LiveTrack and incident detection, and is compatible with Varia cycling devices and inReach satellite communicators. The Edge 1050 provides road hazard alerts, in-ride messaging, and leaderboards for group rides. This move expands Garmin's offerings for cyclists and aims to enhance their riding experience. The company has been successful in the cycling market with products like the Edge 1040 Solar, featuring solar charging and multi-band GNSS technology. This new smart cycling computer is set to cater to the growing demand among cycle enthusiasts.

news.headline: Greenbrier Companies is a reliable dividend stock with high returns and steady cash flow for income investors.
news.summary: Generating significant returns from financial portfolios is the goal for many investors, whether it involves stocks, bonds, ETFs, or other securities. However, for income investors, the emphasis lies in creating a steady cash flow from various liquid investments. This can be achieved through bond interest, interest from diverse investments, and dividends, which are distributions of a company's earnings to shareholders. Typically, dividends are evaluated based on their dividend yield, calculated as a percentage of the current stock price. Research indicates that dividends play a substantial role in long-term returns, sometimes exceeding one-third of the total returns. An example of a company in this context is Greenbrier Companies, headquartered in Lake Oswego.

news.headline: Arbitrum's cryptocurrency (CRYPTO: ARB) plunges 3.37% to $0.80 in 24 hours, down 1.0% over the week.
news.summary: Arbitrum (CRYPTO: ARB) has seen a 3.37% decline in its price in the last 24 hours, dropping to $0.80. This continues the downward trend observed over the past week, with a decrease of 1.0% from $0.81. The price movement and volatility comparison chart for Arbitrum in the past day shows fluctuations. For more details, the full story is accessible on Benzinga.com.

news.headline: Master class on organizing narrative & breaking news stories with award-winning journalists on Aug 16 in Washington.
news.summary: On June 28, 2024, in Washington D.C., an event is set to take place featuring four journalists discussing effective story structuring. This event aims to provide guidance on managing reporting notes, quotes, and data, which can often be daunting for reporters. As stories typically consist of a beginning, middle, and end, journalists are faced with the challenge of choosing the most suitable structure for their pieces. This discussion will shed light on the various options available to journalists in shaping their narratives.

news.headline: Constellation Brands (NYSE: STZ) expected to report Q1 2025 earnings of $3.46/share on $2.7B revenues, with top and bottom-line growth anticipated. Brand portfolio and premiumization efforts driving performance.
news.summary: Constellation Brands, Inc. (NYSE: STZ) is set to report their first-quarter fiscal 2025 results on July 3, 2024. Analysts expect the company to show growth in both revenue and earnings. The Zacks Consensus Estimate for earnings is $3.46 per share, an 18.9% increase from the previous year. Revenue is estimated at $2.7 billion, a 6.3% rise from the same period last year. In the last reported quarter, the company delivered a 7.6% earnings surprise. Constellation Brands' success is attributed to their strong brand portfolio, premiumization efforts, and growth in beer sales, particularly with Modelo Especial and Corona Extra brands. The company's high-end Power Brands in the Wine & Spirits segment, like The Prisoner Brand Family and Kim Crawford, have been key growth drivers.

news.headline: CMS Energy's subsidiary partners with Jupiter Power to build 100MW battery storage in Michigan, moving towards net-zero carbon goal by 2040.Plans to spend $3.4 billion on clean energy between 2024-2028, including energy storage, making up 20% of capital expenditure.
news.summary: CMS Energy Corp.'s subsidiary, Consumers Energy, partners with Jupiter Power to construct 100 Megawatts of battery energy storage projects in Michigan, aligning with its net-zero carbon emission goal by 2040. This move responds to the US demand for clean electricity, reflecting the shift from fossil fuels to renewables. CMS Energy plans to invest $3.4 billion in clean energy from 2024-2028, including energy storage, comprising 20% of its overall five-year capital spending. This initiative reflects the company's commitment to expanding utility-scale renewable energy and energy storage capabilities.

news.headline: Airspan secures up to $95 million in new equity financing, and wipes out all existing funded debt with successful confirmation.
news.summary: Airspan Networks Holdings Inc., a company specializing in innovative software and hardware for 5G networks and a leader in Open RAN and private network solutions, revealed that the United States Bankruptcy Court for the District of Delaware approved its Plan of Reorganization. This move sets the Company up for future growth with a more robust financial position.

news.headline: Citi requests dismissal of retaliation suit filed by former compliance employee.
University of Phoenix study examines invisible labor of working moms. White paper highlights findings from 2023 M.O.M. report.
news.summary: University of Phoenix College of Doctoral Studies has published a new white paper called “Invisible Labor: An Analysis of Moms’ Perceptions in the Workplace” by Joy Taylor, Ed.D., an associate faculty member at the College. Taylor is also a senior research fellow at the Center for Workplace Diversity and Inclusion Research (CWDIR). The paper delves into insights from the 2023 Mothers Overcome More™ or M.O.M.™ report regarding the hidden work undertaken by employed mothers.

Info-Tech Research Group provides wholesale industry with roadmap for AI, data, and Exponential IT adoption amid upheavals.
news.summary: Amidst challenges in the industry and increasing competition, a recent report by Info-Tech Research Group introduces wholesale industry leaders to a guide on incorporating Exponential IT principles. These principles focus on quick decision-making, revamping infrastructure, and utilizing AI technology.
